feed.title: Sandor Dargo's Blog
feed.subtitle: Sandor Dargo writes about about C++, software development, books and stoic philosophy applied to software craftsmanship

entry.title: Variadic class template arguments
entry.summary: Let’s talk about class templates and variadic parameters. How to use them in combination? But first of all, what are variadic templates? Variadic template don’t accept a fixed size of parameters, but anything from one to more. The set of parameters is called the parameter pack. In the template parameter list, we can see the three dots (...) signaling the pack after the typename or class keyw...

entry.title: C++26: Disallow Binding a Returned Reference to a Temporary

link.title: C++26: Disallow Binding a Returned Reference to a Temporary

entry.summary: In short, thanks to P2748R5 by Brian Bi, it’s no longer possible to return a reference that binds to a temporary expression, and that’s just lovely. What exactly is changing? Often, a proposal modifies a lot of wording, and it’s not practical to start by reading through it all. But in this case, the changes are small and easy to digest, so let’s dive in. This line is being removed: “The ...

entry.title: What Makes a C++ Talk Stand Out in the Review Process?

link.title: What Makes a C++ Talk Stand Out in the Review Process?

entry.summary: Let’s talk about getting into conferences. I’ve already shared my path into conference speaking, and more recently, Andre Schau Knatten wrote about how to become a conference speaker. As the question has popped up a few times — especially around Meeting C++ Online — I want to dive into one specific part of the journey: how to write a talk proposal that stands out during the review process. T...

entry.title: Three types of name lookups in C++

link.title: Three types of name lookups in C++

entry.summary: Let’s get back to some basics this week and talk about name lookups in C++. In other words: when you refer to a symbol in your code, how does the compiler find it? Essentially, we can differentiate between three kinds of lookups: Qualified name lookup Unqualified name lookup Argument-Dependent Lookup (ADL) Let’s explore them in that order. Qualified Name Lookup The term qualified r...

entry.title: Constructing Containers from Ranges in C++23

link.title: Constructing Containers from Ranges in C++23

entry.summary: I’ve written plenty on this blog about standard algorithms, but far less about ranges. That’s mostly because, although I’ve had production-ready compilers with C++20 ranges since late 2021, the original ranges library lacked a few key capabilities. The biggest gap was at the end of a pipeline: you could transform data lazily, but you couldn’t drop the result straight into a brand-new container...
